quick_skim.title: Towards Spatial Supersensing in the Wild
quick_skim.abstract: Humans can efficiently parse continuous sensory streams, from hours to years, scaffolding an internal world model that grounds spatial reasoning and prediction. To mimic this capacity, spatial supersensing challenges multimodal models to move beyond linguistic understanding toward true world modeling. However, their benchmark relies on synthetic long videos, formed by concatenating random short clips, and is mostly limited to household scenes, leaving real-world continuity and diversity underexplored. To address the gap, we introduce $\textbf{VSI-Super-Wild}$, a large-scale benchmark for evaluating spatial supersensing over long temporal horizons in diverse in-the-wild scenes. Notably, inspired by cognitive studies on how humans structure experience, we systematically probe the full triad of world state: the agent (observer), objects (scene items), and the environment (places and global layout). In total, VSI-Super-Wild contains $\textbf{6,980}$ human-verified question-answer pairs derived from $\textbf{442}$ real-world videos spanning 8 scene categories, including long-form recordings exceeding 4 hours. Results on VSI-Super-Wild expose a fundamental disconnect: despite advances in static image understanding, models consistently fail at tasks that require coherent world-state tracking over time. We characterize how performance degrades with world-state complexity and temporal horizon, and diagnose four failure modes: spatial collapse, semantic shortcuts, insufficient update, and instance confusion. This taxonomy reveals that models lack mechanisms to bind objects, agents, and environments into a unified spatial world model, a fundamental gap that defines the path forward for spatial supersensing.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for evaluating world models in spatial reasoning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于评估空间推理中世界模型的基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于评估空间推理中世界模型的基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于评估空间推理中世界模型的基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This work introduces VSI-Super-Wild, a large-scale benchmark for evaluating spatial supersensing and world modeling capabilities over long temporal horizons in diverse real-world scenes.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有空间超感知基准依赖合成长视频且局限于家庭场景。本文提出VSI-Super-Wild，一个大规模在野基准，用于评估多模态模型在长时间跨度、多样化真实场景中的世界建模和空间推理能力。基准受认知科学启发，系统探测模型对连续体验的结构化能力。实验揭示当前模型在真实世界连续性和多样性方面的不足，为世界模型研究提供了更具挑战性的评估平台。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有空间超感知基准依赖合成长视频且局限于家庭场景。本文提出VSI-Super-Wild，一个大规模在野基准，用于评估多模态模型在长时间跨度、多样化真实场景中的世界建模和空间推理能力。基准受认知科学启发，系统探测模型对连续体验的结构化能力。实验揭示当前模型在真实世界连续性和多样性方面的不足，为世界模型研究提供了更具挑战性的评估平台。
